title: Lightly Yeasty Mexican Sweet Red Peppers Tlayudas
description: A yeasty, silky, meal-prep Mexican dish featuring sweet red peppers, yakimono-grill, vegetarian-friendly, in the form of tlayudas, cooked on an iron-teppan.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine warm water and yeast, stir to dissolve, and let it sit for 5 minutes.
Add flour, salt, sugar, and 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil to the bowl, mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 4 equal parts and roll each part into a ball.
Using a rolling pin, flatten each ball into a thin circle.
Heat the iron-teppan over medium heat and brush with the remaining vegetable oil.
Place a dough circle on the teppan and cook for 1 minute on each side.
Repeat the cooking process with the remaining dough circles.
Grill the sweet red peppers on the teppan until the skin is charred and blistered.
Peel the skin off the peppers and slice them into thin strips.
Top each cooked tlayuda with sliced peppers, onion, garlic, and oregano.
